what_it_does: Implements the FastMCP agent-facing server (C7) for the Company substrate. It exposes generic verbs over the shared runtime Suite, registers file-discovered modular tools, tags tools with a remote security posture, and provides a large cognition-engine surface (roles, runs, cascades, corpus capture, relations, marks) plus graph and approval-inbox tools.
observations: Uses a lazy Suite proxy (_LazySuite) so importing the module does not construct a second Suite; build_mcp(suite) can bind a caller-supplied Suite before any tool fires.
Declares a SAFE = ToolAnnotations(posture='safe') tag for read-only/low-harm tools; untagged tools default to operator-only.
Discovers modular tools from mcp_face/tools/* via pkgutil and registers them at import time.
Implements graph verbs (connect, set_config, run_graph, get_state, get_results), surfaced-approval reads, self-change audit, events, now, chat, inbox, and capabilities.

Provides a broad cognition face: inspect (cognition_info, models_for_role, cognition_inputs, field_types, list_skills_contexts, inspect_address, reduce_rule_names), configure+run (run_role, run_draft, run_items, run_draft_items, run_reduce, save_cascade, list_cascades, run_cascade, preview_turn), authoring (propose_role, edit_role, delete_role), corpus (capture, find_relations), and marks (mark).
Wraps every registered tool with _guard_tools to re-raise unexpected exceptions as labelled internal errors while letting ValueError/KeyError teaching errors pass through.
Contains extensive inline design commentary and cross-references to issues/gaps (G1, C7, C9.2, N7, etc.).
standouts: The _LazySuite + build_mcp factory is the central shared-layer mechanism that lets the bridge and stdio/remote faces use the same tool manager without a second Suite at import.
Posture tagging via ToolAnnotations(posture='safe') is used as an inline, registry-native security classification for the remote gateway.
The cognition-engine face exposes run_role, run_items, run_reduce, run_cascade, and capture as thin wrappers over runtime.cognition and Suite methods, emphasizing reuse-don't-parallel.
_guard_tools provides a single after-registration error guard that distinguishes contract errors from internal bugs.
conventions: Follows a 'parameterised tool' consolidation pattern (one noun with an op selector) and references MCP-DESIGN-PRINCIPLE.
Heavy inline documentation explaining design rationale, floor constraints, and issue references.
Fail-loud on unknown ids, malformed specs, conflicting Suite rebinds, and unresolvable addresses.
Uses registry-is-truth: derives tool schemas, reduce-rule names, and capability sections from live registries rather than hardcoded lists.
Reuses Suite/runtime.cognition engine paths instead of reimplementing them for the MCP face.
concerns: Despite file-discovered modular tools, server.py remains a very large module with many inline tool definitions and long docstrings.
_LazySuite relies on a class-level _real variable and transparent __getattr__/__dir__; thread-safety and rebinding semantics are not explicitly addressed.
_guard_tools mutates mcp._tool_manager._tools private attributes, creating coupling to FastMCP internals.
Dynamic sys.path insertion and pkgutil-based imports run at module load, which can produce side effects during import.
Some function signatures and docstrings are extremely long, increasing maintenance surface.
notes: This is the shared MCP tool surface: it can be imported safely (no Suite constructed) and then bound to an existing Suite so the bridge and stdio server share the same tools.
questions: What concrete modules are discovered under mcp_face/tools and what verbs do they register?
How does the remote gateway (mcp_face/remote.py) actually read and enforce the posture annotation?
What is the exact contract of the Suite methods that these tools delegate to?
How are mark_types, projections, and generation_policies registered and validated?
Is _LazySuite safe if multiple threads call tools concurrently before or during Suite construction?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose — a generic, node-type-agnostic FastMCP face over the shared Suite — matches the implementation; the lazy proxy and posture tags are added mechanisms to support that purpose safely.
apparent_themes: shared substrate between human and agent faces
lazy initialization to avoid duplicate runtime objects
fail-closed security via inline posture tags
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the MCP agent face a first-class, safe peer of the UI bridge: same Suite, same tools, same engine, with clear boundaries around what an agent can do unilaterally versus what requires operator approval.
novelty: The lazy Suite proxy that defers construction until first use and can be rebound to a caller-supplied Suite is a distinctive solution to the 'second Suite at import' problem; combining FastMCP annotations with a custom posture field for tiered remote gating is also unusual.
contribution: Provides a bindable, posture-tagged MCP tool surface that exposes graph operations, cognition runs, cascades, corpus capture, and surfaced-approval reads, all delegated to the shared Suite/runtime engine rather than reimplemented.
summary_for_embedding: FastMCP server module exposing Suite-backed generic tools for graph manipulation, cognition runs, cascades, corpus capture, and approval inbox, using a lazy Suite proxy and posture-tagged security annotations.
intention_for_embedding: To give the agent MCP face the same runtime substrate as the UI bridge while preventing duplicate Suite construction and keeping operator-gated mutations off the agent surface.

symbols.does: Transparent lazy proxy that defers Suite construction until first attribute access.
symbols.description: A class that stands in for a real Suite instance so module-level tool decorators can capture SUITE without triggering Suite construction at import time; it can also be explicitly bound to a caller-supplied Suite via build_mcp.

symbols.does: Constructs the default Suite if none has been bound.
symbols.description: Class method that lazily builds a Suite from FsStore and a discovered NodeRegistry only when a tool first dereferences an attribute through the proxy.

symbols.does: Forwards attribute lookups to the real Suite.
symbols.description: Only called for names not found on the proxy itself; resolves through _ensure() so tool bodies hit the bound or default Suite at call time.

symbols.does: Returns dir(Suite) for schema derivation without constructing the Suite.
symbols.description: Used by modular create tooling to enumerate create_<kind> method names at decoration time; because these are class methods on Suite, the name set matches a real instance while _real stays None.

symbols.does: Binds a caller-supplied Suite into the lazy proxy and returns the FastMCP server.
symbols.description: The shared-layer factory: allows the bridge to pass its own Suite so the same tool closures dispatch against it; fails loudly on a conflicting rebind.

symbols.does: Returns the full node-type library projection.
symbols.description: Read-only tool tagged safe; delegates to SUITE.object_info() for rendering and composition metadata about registered node types.

symbols.does: Lists node types that produce a given port type.
symbols.description: Read-only safe tool; delegates to SUITE.list_by_type(output_type) for type-graph queries.

symbols.does: Lists all graphs in the substrate.
symbols.description: Read-only safe tool; returns the set of canvases via SUITE.list_graphs().

symbols.does: Wires a connection between two node ports in a graph.
symbols.description: Mutating tool (untagged/operator-only) that calls SUITE.connect to create an edge from from_node.from_port to to_node.to_port.

symbols.does: Updates a node's config in a graph.
symbols.description: Mutating tool that applies SUITE.set_config with a defensive dict() copy of the supplied config.

symbols.does: Runs a graph and returns ran/cached/stuck summaries.
symbols.description: Mutating tool that invokes SUITE.run(graph, branch) and returns a deterministic summary of which nodes ran versus were memoized.

symbols.does: Returns the current state of a graph.
symbols.description: Read-only tool returning nodes, statuses, outputs, and addresses via SUITE.state(graph).

symbols.does: Returns each node's output in a graph.
symbols.description: Read-only tool delegating to SUITE.results(graph).

symbols.does: Queries the surfaced-action / build-approval inbox.
symbols.description: Lists surfaced items with filtering by sid, status, unresolved-only, limit, and detail level; distinguishes this self-growth queue from the operator decisions inbox.

symbols.does: Returns the self-modification audit ledger.
symbols.description: Read-only tool returning recent [self-apply] commits with sha, subject, timestamp, changed files, and revert flag via SUITE.self_change_log.

symbols.does: Returns recent captured trajectory events.
symbols.description: Read-only tool returning recent actions (run, create, connect, grow, approve) newest-first via SUITE.events.

symbols.does: Returns the now-view and presence snapshot for a graph.
symbols.description: Defaults graph to 'codebase' so the tool is invokable without arguments; delegates to SUITE.now(graph).

symbols.does: Converses with the right-hand-man coherent voice.
symbols.description: Calls SUITE.chat and optionally speaks the reply through the bridge's serialized speaker if RHM voice_out is configured for server output.

symbols.does: Returns the chief-of-staff inbox lanes.
symbols.description: Read-only tool returning live escalations, resolved-for-you, and batched lanes via SUITE.inbox_lanes().

symbols.does: Returns the source-of-truth registry projection of what exists.
symbols.description: Read-only safe tool; returns a concise map by default, supports scoped section queries, and adds synthetic 'chains' and 'features' sections derived from the flow registry and design/register.json.

symbols.does: Builds the chains subsection for capabilities.
symbols.description: Inner helper that discovers FlowRegistry flows and lists saved cascades, telling the caller which tool runs each.

symbols.does: Builds the features subsection for capabilities.
symbols.description: Inner helper that loads design/register.json and returns the closed feature inventory used by register_element's no-fiction contract.

symbols.does: Adapts cognition engine emit payloads onto the Suite event log.
symbols.description: Thin sink passed to run_items/run_reduce; wraps SUITE._emit with a summary derived from the payload.

symbols.does: Resolves a registered role id or a draft field-set into a fireable Role.
symbols.description: For strings, validates against SUITE.role_registry; for dicts, renders and loads from a temp module via authoring helpers, then removes the tempdir. Fail-loud on unknown ids or bad specs.

symbols.does: Inspects the cognition registries.
symbols.description: Read-only safe tool returning a concise overview, one section in full, or one role's spec including prompt_template and round-trippable output_fields.

symbols.does: Builds an output-field row from a Pydantic model field.
symbols.description: Inner helper that converts Pydantic annotations to registry type names (str, int, bool, enum with values, list[...]) so inspect output can be fed back into create(kind='role').

symbols.does: Looks up models matching a role's requirements or explicit capability strings.
symbols.description: Read-only safe tool; resolves a role's model_binding.requires and delegates to SUITE.models_for_role.

symbols.does: Lists available input addresses for a role or model.
symbols.description: Read-only safe tool projecting SUITE.available_inputs against a role-resolved model or an explicit model id.

symbols.does: Returns the closed output-schema field-type registry.
symbols.description: Read-only safe tool delegating to SUITE.field_types().

symbols.does: Lists addressable skills, contexts, and guides.
symbols.description: Read-only safe tool that reads runtime.cognition skill/context/guide registries and reports staleness for guides.

symbols.does: Formats registry entries as id/label/description rows.
symbols.description: Inner helper used by list_skills_contexts for skills and contexts.

symbols.does: Resolves, lists, or attaches a page face to an addressed thing.
symbols.description: Multi-op tool: default reads an address via resolve_address; 'attach_page' stores HTML and binds it; 'pages' lists current page faces.

symbols.does: Fires a single registered role and persists its output.
symbols.description: Resolves the role, resolves address-valued inputs, calls _cog.run_role, persists the validated output to run://<turn>/<role>, and emits an op.run index record.

symbols.does: Shared tail for single-role fire-and-persist.
symbols.description: Used by run_role and run_draft; mints turn_id, resolves inputs, fires the role, persists output, emits op.run record, and returns the agent-facing shape.

symbols.does: Fires a one-off inline role spec without registering it.
symbols.description: Resolves a draft role dict into a temp-module Role, fires it once via _fire_role_and_persist, and never writes or commits a roles/ file.

symbols.does: Fans one role over N input units.
symbols.description: MAP primitive: resolves the role, runs _cog.run_items, and returns addresses, resolved values, finish order, skipped units, and wall time.

symbols.does: Shared tail for the map fan-and-shape.
symbols.description: Used by run_items and run_draft_items; mints turn_id, fans via _cog.run_items, and returns the agent-facing ItemsResult shape.

symbols.does: Fans a one-off inline role spec over N input units.
symbols.description: Draft MAP primitive: resolves a draft role dict without committing it, then reuses _run_items_and_shape for the fan.

symbols.does: Reduces a set of run:// addresses into one output.
symbols.description: JOIN primitive supporting role-based synthesis, named deterministic reduce rules, or embed clustering; delegates to _cog.run_reduce and labels input pairs for readability.

symbols.does: Lists the named deterministic reduce rules.
symbols.description: Read-only safe tool deriving names from the single-source _cog.REDUCE_RULES dict, including the parameterised tally-by:<field> pattern.

symbols.does: Saves a multi-step pipeline declaration as a cascade.
symbols.description: Validates the declaration through SUITE.save_cascade and persists it if valid; fail-loud on malformed declarations.

symbols.does: Lists saved cascade recipes.
symbols.description: Read-only safe tool returning the full declarations of saved cascades via SUITE.list_cascades().

symbols.does: Runs a saved cascade end-to-end.
symbols.description: Loads the saved decl and delegates to SUITE.run_cascade, threading step outputs into inputs via run:// addresses.

symbols.does: Runs a staged chat turn without mutating history.
symbols.description: Non-mutating tool that reuses SUITE.preview_turn to show how an utterance would be routed through casts and rules.

symbols.does: Proposes a new role and surfaces it for operator approval.
symbols.description: Authoring tool that renders and gates a role module, then surfaces it via SUITE.propose_role without direct application.

symbols.does: Proposes an edit to an existing role.
symbols.description: Authoring tool that re-proposes an existing role for operator approval; protected roles refuse.

symbols.does: Requests removal of a role.
symbols.description: Authoring tool that surfaces a role deletion for operator approval; protected roles refuse.

symbols.does: Fans a role over corpus units and persists durable corpus records.
symbols.description: Layer 1 of the corpus pipeline: runs _cog.run_items over units, then hands per-unit outputs to SUITE.capture_corpus for lineage-gated record writes and optional embedding into a projection space.

symbols.does: Queries cross-space inversions over the vector index.
symbols.description: Layer 3 inversion-finder: returns items near the given item in near_space but not near it in far_space, using a cosine threshold; delegates to SUITE.find_relations.

symbols.does: Appends a typed mark on a claim or span.
symbols.description: Write tool for the marks layer; validates mark_type against the registry and appends a mark record via SUITE.mark, carrying optional value, confidence, source_pass, and evidence.

symbols.does: Wraps every registered tool with an internal-error guard.
symbols.description: After-registration loop that replaces each tool function with a wrapper letting ValueError/KeyError pass through but re-raising other exceptions as labelled internal errors.

symbols.does: Factory that creates a guarded wrapper for a named tool.
symbols.description: Inner closure generator used by _guard_tools to bind the original function and tool name.

symbols.does: The actual guarded wrapper around a tool function.
symbols.description: Catches ValueError/KeyError unchanged and converts any other exception into a RuntimeError naming the tool and exception type.
